title: [rootless] error: cannot mount using driver overlay in rootless mode
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

_podman_ gives an error that it can't mount using driver overlay in rootless mode, but it should, since there's _fuse-overlayfs_.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman mount -a`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
INFO[0000] running as rootless
DEBU[0000] Initializing boltdb state at /home/podman/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod/bolt_state.db
DEBU[0000] Using graph driver overlay
DEBU[0000] Using graph root /home/podman/.local/share/containers/storage
DEBU[0000] Using run root /tmp/run-1001
DEBU[0000] Using static dir /home/podman/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod
DEBU[0000] Using tmp dir /tmp/run-1001/libpod/tmp
DEBU[0000] Using volume path /home/podman/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
DEBU[0000] Set libpod namespace to ""
DEBU[0000] Not configuring container store
ERRO[0000] cannot mount using driver overlay in rootless mode
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
INFO[0000] running as rootless
Version:            1.2.0-dev
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.11.5
Git Commit:         232b46a3741267d8dd77a81196699d551c7a682e
Built:              Wed Mar 20 19:40:30 2019
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
INFO[0000] running as rootless
DEBU[0000] Initializing boltdb state at /home/podman/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod/bolt_state.db
DEBU[0000] Using graph driver overlay
DEBU[0000] Using graph root /home/podman/.local/share/containers/storage
DEBU[0000] Using run root /tmp/run-1001

DEBU[0000] Set libpod namespace to ""
DEBU[0000] [graphdriver] trying provided driver "overlay"
DEBU[0000] overlay: mount_program=/home/podman/podman/usr/local/bin/fuse-overlayfs
DEBU[0000] backingFs=extfs, projectQuotaSupported=false, useNativeDiff=false, usingMetacopy=false
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: 232b46a3741267d8dd77a81196699d551c7a682e
  go version: go1.11.5
  podman version: 1.2.0-dev
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.8-dev
  Conmon:

    version: 'conmon version , commit: 84c860029893e2e2dd71d62231f009c9dcd3c0b4'
  Distribution:
    distribution: debian
    version: "9"
  MemFree: 33760960512

      commit: 2b18fe1d885ee5083ef9f0838fee39b62d653e30

  os: linux
  rootless: true
  uptime: 273h 34m 5.37s (Approximately 11.38 days)

  registries: []

  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
```

comments.body:
we can mount use fuse-overlayfs but that can be done only inside a user namespace.

A rootless container cannot mount on the host mount namespace, so it cannot honor the `podman mount` semantic where you'll see the container content mounted locally.

More details here: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/1225

If you need to mount the container image, you can use `buildah unshare + buildah mount`.

I talk about this in this blog.
https://opensource.com/article/19/3/tips-tricks-rootless-buildah
`podman mount` used to work in Fedora 29 until some update, I think. What't the current examples/suggestion to use `podman mount` (not `buildah` stuff)?
